input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Xcode (iOS builds)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- **Version discovery:** MAUI release notes specify the minimum Xcode version. Check [github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/wiki) and the workload manifest.
- After install, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select -s /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -runFirstLaunch
  xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- **Verify:** `xcodebuild -version`

## 2. .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** release that supports MAUI.
- **Version discovery:** The [MAUI release notes](https://github.com/dotnet/maui/releases) and [NuGet package page](https://www.nuget.org/packages/Microsoft.Maui.Sdk) list the required minimum .NET SDK version.
- Use the **macOS arm64 (Apple Silicon)** or **x64** installer as appropriate.
- **Verify:** `dotnet --version`

## 3. .NET MAUI Workload

- Install after the SDK:
  ```
  sudo dotnet workload install maui
  ```
- This pulls in sub-workloads: `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, `android`, `ios`.
- **Verify:** `dotnet workload list` — all four should appear.

## 4. Android SDK & JDK

The `maui-android` workload auto-installs the Android SDK and a compatible JDK under `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/`. You do **not** need Android Studio, but if you want emulator management UI:

- **Source:** [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
- The workload pins specific Android API levels and build-tools versions. Discover them with:
  ```
  dotnet workload search android
  grep -r "android" ~/.dotnet/sdk-manifests/
  ```
- To use a custom Android SDK location:
  ```
  export ANDROID_HOME=$HOME/Library/Android/sdk
  ```
- **Verify:** `dotnet build -t:ResolveAndroidTooling -v:n` in any MAUI project.

## 5. iOS Simulators

- After Xcode is installed:
  ```
  xcrun simctl list devices available
  ```
- Download additional runtimes via **Xcode → Settings → Platforms**.

## 6. Android Emulator

```
$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager "emulator" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/avdmanager create avd -n maui_test -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;arm64-v8a"
emulator -avd maui_test
```

Adjust the API level (`android-35`) to match what the workload installed.

## 7. IDE (pick one)

| IDE | Source |
|---|---|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** (full MAUI support) | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

## 8. End-to-End Verification

```bash
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# iOS Simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-ios \
  -p:_DeviceName=:v2:udid=$(xcrun simctl list -j devices available \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json;d=json.load(sys.stdin);print([u['udid'] for r in d['devices'].values() for u in r if u['isAvailable']][0])")

# Android Emulator (emulator must be running)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net9.0-android
```

Both should launch the default MAUI template app.

## Version Compatibility Cheat-Sheet

Always cross-reference these three sources before installing:

1. **MAUI release notes** → required .NET SDK + Xcode versions
2. **Xcode release notes** → supported macOS versions
3. **Android workload manifest** (`~/.dotnet/sdk-manifests/`) → pinned Android SDK / JDK

This ensures all components are compatible with each other.
